descriptions: BMF had direct links to Mexican drug cartels for cocaine supply.
edges.evidence_quote: By 2000, it had established cocaine distribution sales throughout the United States through its Los Angeles–based drug source and direct links to Mexican drug cartels.

edges.evidence_quote: They were allied with several Mexican and Colombian drug cartels

descriptions: Solntsevskaya out-gunned Mexican drug cartels in Atlanta, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Although not as large as the drug cartels of Mexico that have sent men to try and set up shop in Atlanta, Odenussa and his cohorts have out-gunned the Mexican drug cartels and the African American gangs in Atlanta.

descriptions: Texis Cartel worked with Mexican drug cartels.

edges.evidence_quote: It contains two sub-cliques known as the Jr Black Angels and Black Angels.

Plug Uglies.description: The Plug Uglies were an American Nativist criminal street gang active in Baltimore, Maryland from 1854 to 1865. They were known for their involvement in election-day violence and street battles, often using oversized plug hats as helmets. The gang participated in the 1856 Baltimore Know-Nothing Riot and the 1863 New York City Draft Riots. They were allied with the Know Nothing political party and engaged in assassinations, shootings, and rioting.

descriptions: Bloody Tubs were allied with Plug Uglies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Plug Uglies, Rip Raps, Nativists, Dead Rabbits

descriptions: Infobox lists Plug Uglies as rivals of Bowery Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Dead Rabbits, Plug Uglies, Roach Guards, Shirt Tails, Chichesters, Tammany Hall

descriptions: Plug Uglies listed as ally of Dead Rabbits in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Chichesters, Tammany Hall, Plug Uglies, Roach Guards, Mulberry Street Boys, Municipal Police, Forty Thieves, Shirt Tails, Kerryonians

descriptions: Plug Uglies and Bloody Tubs both participated in the 1863 Draft Riots.
edges.evidence_quote: The New York Times reported that Plug-Uglies and Bloody Tubs gang members from Baltimore... had come to New York to participate in the riots

descriptions: Plug Uglies allied with Dead Rabbits during the 1863 Draft Riots.
edges.evidence_quote: They allied themselves with the New York City Irish Dead Rabbits gang in looting New York City during the American Civil War in the Draft Riots of 1863.

descriptions: Plug Uglies and Rip Raps jointly participated in the 1856 election riot.
edges.evidence_quote: Together with the Rip Raps, they were also actively involved in deadly rioting at the October 1856 municipal election in Baltimore

descriptions: Plug Uglies and Schuykill Rangers both participated in the 1863 Draft Riots.
edges.evidence_quote: the Philadelphia Schuykill Rangers under Jimmy Haggerty and other rowdies of Philadelphia, had come to New York to participate in the riots

'Ndrina Facchineri.description: Historical 'ndrina based in Cittanova, Calabria, operating since the late 19th century. Engages in extortion, commercial activities, drug trafficking, and past heroin trafficking and kidnappings. Involved in feuds with rival clans, notably the Raso-Albanese-Gullace group. Has expanded to other Italian regions including Puglia, Umbria, and Lazio.

descriptions: In the 1960s, Albanese together with Raso and Gullace clashed with Facchineri, Marvaso, and Monteleone.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni sessanta a Cittanova insieme ai Raso e ai Gullace si sono scontrati con i Facchineri , i Marvaso e i Monteleone.

descriptions: Facchineri and Albanese were opposing sides in the Faida di Cittanova.
edges.evidence_quote: Faida di Cittanova : originatasi probabilmente nel 1964 quando venne ucciso un parente della 'ndrina dei Facchineri i quali risposero uccidendo un membro degli Albanese

descriptions: Facchineri-Varone are in conflict with Albanese in the first faida.
edges.evidence_quote: la prima faida fra i Facchineri-Varone... e i Raso, gli Albanese, i Gullace e i De Raco

descriptions: Avignone supports Facchineri-Varone in the first faida.
edges.evidence_quote: I primi sono sostenuti anche dagli Avignone

edges.evidence_quote: la prima faida fra i Facchineri-Varone... e i Raso, gli Albanese, i Gullace e i De Raco

edges.evidence_quote: La faida continuò fino al 1980 e vide la sconfitta dei Facchineri a opera degli Albanese-Raso-Gullace

I Belfiore , i Tripodo , i Mesiani Mazzacuva, i Ruga , gli Aquino , gli Ierinò , i Macrì , i Morabito , i Varacalli , i Cataldo , gli Strangio , i Barbaro , gli Alvaro , i Palamara , i Raso-Albanese e i Facchineri .

Shui Fong.description: Shui Fong is a major Triad group based in Hong Kong, founded in the 1930s from a workers' union of a soft drink company. It operates in Hong Kong, Macau, mainland China, and Chinese communities abroad, engaging in extortion, loan sharking, narcotics, and control of nightclubs and mahjong dens. It is one of Hong Kong's most active triads and part of Macau's 'Four Major Gangs'. The group has been involved in violent conflicts with rivals like 14K.

descriptions: Wo On Lok is a rival of 14K per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Ah Kong , Big Circle Gang , Sun Yee On , Wo On Lok , Wo Shing Wo

descriptions: Shui Fong and 14K were engaged in a gang war in Macau during the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: They were involved in a high-profile gang war with perennial rivals the 14K, led in Macau by 'Broken Tooth Koi' during this period.

descriptions: The infobox lists 14K as a rival of Shui Fong.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: 14K, Wo Shing Wo

descriptions: Both Wo On Lok and Sun Yee On fled to Britain around the same time due to the same event, but no direct relationship between them is stated.
edges.evidence_quote: Following the signing of the Sino-British Joint Declaration between the UK and China in 1984, the news of Hong Kong's return to China caused many Triads to flee to Britain, specifically the Wo On Lok and the Sun Yee On .

descriptions: The infobox lists Wo Shing Wo as a rival of Shui Fong.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: 14K, Wo Shing Wo

descriptions: Shui Fong member Philip Wong was murdered after refusing to do business with Wo Shing Wo, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: It is believed Wong was murdered after refusing to do business with the Wo Shing Wo who wanted a share of his lucrative Chinese video rental business.

descriptions: Infobox lists Wo On Lok as an ally of Sun Yee On.

descriptions: Midlands Outlaws formed as a coalition against Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: The various gangs united to become the Midlands Outlaws, whose 'colours' consisted of a skull wearing Native American headdress featuring seven different coloured feathers, with each colour representing one of the founding clubs.

descriptions: Midlands Outlaws patched over to become part of Outlaws MC.
edges.evidence_quote: The Midlands Outlaws later began 'prospecting' for the American Outlaws Association — the Hells Angels' biggest rival internationally — and 'patched over' to join the A.O.A. in February 2000.

Bloods.description: The Bloods are a predominantly African-American street gang founded in Los Angeles, California in 1972. They are known for their red color, hand signs spelling 'Blood', and rivalry with the Crips. The gang consists of numerous subsets called 'sets' and is involved in drug trafficking, extortion, murder, robbery, and pimping. They have spread across the United States and influenced similar groups in Europe.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Avenues, Mara Salvatrucha, Playboys, Santa Monica 13, Temple Street (gang), Vineland Boys, Florencia 13, Bloods, Black P. Stones (Jungles)

edges.evidence_quote: Along with the Crips and Bloods , Kumi 415 members have recently provided recruitment pools for the Black Guerrilla Family , a gang with similarly large numbers in the Bay area and Northern California .

descriptions: Belizean offshoots retained rivalries along Bloods vs Crips lines.
edges.evidence_quote: When the first street gangs began to splinter, the offshoots originally retained strong allegiances to (and so strong rivalries with) Bloods or Crips.

edges.evidence_quote: Bloods and Crips gangs were introduced to Belize by US deportees in the 1980s, and especially adopted by locals upon the 1988 release of Colors .

descriptions: Bloods formed to counter the Crips.
edges.evidence_quote: several independent street gangs in Los Angeles formed an alliance known as the Bloods to counter the growing influence of the Crips

Oficina de Envigado.description: Colombian drug cartel and criminal organization originally founded as an enforcement wing and debt collection service for Pablo Escobar's Medellín Cartel. Founded by Diego Murillo Bejarano (Don Berna) in the early 1990s. It operates mainly in Medellín and Envigado, controlling extortion, gambling, and money laundering. By 2019, its activities shifted from direct drug trafficking to providing services to lower-level traffickers and mafia groups.

descriptions: Oficina de Envigado is listed among Bacrim that emerged after AUC demobilization, indicating a successor relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: Clan Úsuga ( Clan del Golfo o Autodefensas Gaitanistas ), Los Rastrojos , el Ejército Revolucionario Popular Antisubversivo de Colombia (ERPAC) , las Águilas Negras , Los Paisas , la Oficina de Envigado y otras facciones menores como Los Machos y Renacer

descriptions: The AGC clashed with the Oficina de Envigado over territory.
edges.evidence_quote: Rendón attempted to expand his empire, moving into southern Córdoba, the Lower Cauca region in northern Antioquia and even venturing into Medellin, long controlled by the Oficina de Envigado.

descriptions: The north side Popes are allied with Imperial Gangsters per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Folk Nation , TAP Boyz , Imperial Gangsters , Simon City Royals

descriptions: Imperial Gangsters listed as rival in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Sureños, 12st Players, Almighty Bishops, 4GMz, SVLz, Latin Kings, Two Six, 22Boys, Almighty Ambrose, La Raza, Satan Disciples, Krazy Getdown Boys, Imperial Gangsters, Maniac Latin Disciples, Spanish Gangster Disciples, Insane Deuces, Folk Nation

descriptions: Latin Eagles allied with Imperial Gangsters in the Almighty Folks Familia.
edges.evidence_quote: During the mid-1990s, the Almighty Latin Eagles formed an alliance with the Almighty Imperial Gangsters and Simon City Royals as the Almighty Folks Familia.

descriptions: Imperial Gangsters followed Latin Disciples into the Folks Alliance in 1978 due to ULO.
edges.evidence_quote: With the Latin Disciples joining Folks, the Spanish Cobras, Imperial Gangsters, and Latin Eagles immediately followed due to the United Latino Organization they had on the street.

descriptions: Simon City Royals are part of the Almighty Family faction with Imperial Gangsters.
edges.evidence_quote: This faction consists of the Royals, Imperial Gangsters , Harrison Gents, Latin Eagles and Ambrose.

descriptions: Imperial Gangsters listed as rival in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: People Nation , Latin Kings , Latin Counts , Spanish Cobras , Latin Eagles , Imperial Gangsters, Simon City Royals , Harrison Gents, Almighty Ambrose

descriptions: SGDs had good relationships with Imperial Gangsters through drug sales.
edges.evidence_quote: Drug sales created good relationships with all the Folks in the surrounding areas, including the ... Imperial Gangsters

edges.source: Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine – General Command
descriptions: Arab Palestine Organisation split from PFLP-GC.
edges.evidence_quote: Zarur later split from the PFLP-GC to form the Arab Palestine Organisation.

descriptions: The infobox lists Warlords as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Kings Crew, Rebels, Outcasts, Warlords, RCMP

descriptions: The Grim Reapers were one of four dominant clubs in Alberta, alongside Rebels, Warlords, and King's Crew.
edges.evidence_quote: Along with the Rebels, the Warlords, and King's Crew, they were one of the four dominant outlaw motorcycle clubs operating in Alberta prior to 1997.

descriptions: Satan's Angels MC merged with Hells Angels in 1983.
edges.evidence_quote: The Satan's Angels MC in Vancouver had a black member when it merged with the Hells Angels in 1983.

descriptions: Satan's Angels merged into Hells Angels as full patch members in 1983.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1981, Yves Buteau, the national president of the Hells Angels, approached Satan's Angels with an offer to 'patch over'... On 23 July 1983, the Satan's Angels joined the Hells Angels as 'full patch' members

descriptions: Satan's Angels patched over to become Hells Angels chapter.
edges.evidence_quote: In Western Canada, in 1983 a Vancouver club known as Satan's Angels were patched over to form the first BC chapter.

descriptions: Sacra Corona Libera is a splinter group from SCU.
edges.evidence_quote: Alcuni di loro hanno fondato la Sacra corona libera.

descriptions: Sacra Corona Libera is a splinter group founded by former SCU members.
edges.evidence_quote: La Sacra Corona Libera fu fondata nel 1998 da Massimo D'Amico, Antonio Vitale e Massimo Pasimeni, criminali mesagnesi già appartenuti alla Sacra corona unita.

descriptions: Aprea and Celeste-Guarino fought a faida in 2005-2006.
edges.evidence_quote: faida tra gli Aprea e i Celeste-Guarino : combattuta nella zona di Barra tra il clan Aprea e quella che secondo gli investigatori era la fazione scissionista dei Celeste-Guarino negli anni 2005 e 2006

descriptions: Zoe Pound has Sapp Boys as rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Sapp Boys, MS-13 , and Hells Angels

descriptions: The División del Norte was placed under the command of the Cuerpo de Ejército del Noroeste.
edges.evidence_quote: Formalmente el primer jefe del Ejército Constitucionalista, Venustiano Carranza dispuso que la División del Norte estaría bajo las órdenes del Cuerpo de Ejército del Noroeste
